behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module wiring: manifest parser + dependency graph/lockfile resolver + scanner input assembly. A unit test on one parser function can miss real handoff bugs (e.g., transitive nodes dropped between resolver and query builder). No real network/OS API is required beyond local files.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency discovery against fixture projects (e.g., npm with package-lock, pip with requirements + lock/constraints) and assert the produced dependency set contains expected direct and transitive packages/versions before query dispatch.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real HTTP/API contract semantics for version-range matching. In-process mocks can hide failures where the live API ignores/misparses range filters or expects different encoding. Real infra (network + external API behavior) is part of the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Against a test/sandbox vulnerability API (or controlled real endpoint), submit dependencies with known package names but different vulnerable/non-vulnerable version ranges; assert returned CVEs match only the manifest’s exact versions/ranges and exclude same-package mismatched versions.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary; no I/O or cross-module infrastructure is needed to catch real comparator bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list with mixed severities and equal-severity varying CVSS scores into the sorter and assert exact output order: severity rank first, CVSS descending (or specified direction) second.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is parser/editor/writer handoff on actual manifest files. Unit tests can miss serializer side effects (reordering, whitespace/key loss, accidental edits in other sections). Local filesystem + module wiring is enough; no external systems required.
behaviors.test_description: Apply remediation to fixture manifests on disk; diff original vs updated file and assert only the targeted dependency version token changed, with all other dependency entries and unrelated sections unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Requires real git hosting/API behavior (branch creation, commit, PR open, rendered diff). Mocking PR client can always pass while missing auth, permission, branch protection, or diff-shaping failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end remediation against a real test repository; verify one PR is created, changed files contain only the intended manifest edit, and diff shows a single dependency bump with no unrelated changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver comparison logic at single-function/object boundary; no external infra needed to detect incorrect major-bump classification.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test over semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata and edge cases) asserting `breaking=true` iff target major > current major according to spec used by the scanner.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration across modules: semver decision -> remediation planner -> PR publisher gating -> output/reporting. A unit test on one component can miss wiring regressions (e.g., flag set but PR still sent). External API is not required if PR publisher is a local fake that records calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner flow with a known breaking upgrade candidate using integrated modules and a test double PR publisher; assert zero PR publish attempts and scanner output/report contains the required review-needed flag and reason.
